title: Keybinds & Shortcuts
summary: A cleaner at-a-glance reference for the controls players use most often.
intro: This page now uses a shortcut-focused layout so the most important controls can be scanned quickly instead of getting buried inside regular paragraph sections.

shortcutGroups.title: Essential Omni Shortcuts
shortcutGroups.description: Core actions for movement, interaction, and opening the main overlays during normal play.

entries.description: Use the standard movement cluster to walk and adjust position in combat or exploration.
Hold the sprint key to cover open terrain more quickly.
Use the traversal button for jumps, ledges, and low obstacles.
Talk to characters, pick up items, and use camp stations or doors.
Review gear, quick slots, and any important items you are carrying.
Bring up the current area map and your active objective overview.
Trigger the currently equipped tool, gadget, or context action.
Open the pause menu or close the panel you are currently looking at.

shortcutGroups.title: Combat & Field Actions
shortcutGroups.description: A second group for the more situational buttons that still need to be easy to find mid-session.

entries.description: Use the main equipped action, attack, or tool interaction.
Use an alternate fire mode, aim state, or secondary tool function.
Cycle between quick-slot items without opening the full inventory.
Toggle a support ability used for visibility or clue hunting.

sections.heading: Notes
paragraphs: The shortcut layout is driven entirely by JSON, so you can keep adding rows or split the controls into more groups without changing the HTML.
If you want later, we can also add controller tabs, platform-specific bindings, or little icons for mouse and modifier keys.
items: Each shortcut row supports a title, description, and any number of keys.
Setting showImage to false keeps the page focused on the controls instead of the article hero art.
Other wiki pages still use the regular article layout unless they define their own custom content blocks.
